name: Thyme-Scented Pork Marsala
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from pork; cut each tenderloin crosswise into 1/2-inch slices.
2. Combine flour and salt in a zip-top plastic bag; add pork. Seal bag, and shake well to coat; set aside.
3. Melt 1 tbsp margarine in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add half of the pork; cook 1 1/2 minutes on each side or until browned. Remove pork from skillet; set aside and keep warm. Repeat procedure with remaining margarine and pork.
4. Add wine to skillet, scraping skillet to loosen browned bits. Add thyme and bouillon granules. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat; cook 1 minute. Return pork to skillet; cover. Reduce heat, simmer 2 minutes or until pork is done and sauce is thick.
